mechanism_narrative: An increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are significantly higher when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.5 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real GDP and stable consumer prices indicate a healthy expansionary environment, supporting corporate profit margins and generating positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are lower when both GDPC1 grows and CPIAUCSL remains stable.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects strengthening factory utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive short-term market sentiment and returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 55 percent of periods following an INDPRO acceleration, or the signal loses predictive power during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 0.5 percent combined with rising inflation expectations forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing borrowing costs and suppressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces positive forward returns in over 65 percent of triggered cases, or Rank IC remains above 0.04 in the positive direction.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month gains in total nonfarm payrolls demonstrate robust labor market expansion, which supports household income growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of periods following positive PAYEMS revisions, or the signal shows no statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales alongside increasing housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business investment strength, creating a favorable macro backdrop for equity appreciation.
failure_condition: The combined RSAFS and HOUST uptrend coincides with negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of observations, invalidating the demand synchronization premise.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below the 2017 baseline index of 100, it signals a contraction in manufacturing output and supply-side weakness, leading to negative short-term market returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in over 55 percent of instances where INDPRO drops below 100, or the threshold fails to predict downside risk in walk-forward tests.
